# Select

The real <select>, opted into full styling — rich HTML in options, an animated picker panel, and a mirrored selection. Falls back to the classic native select everywhere else.

```html
<select
  aria-label="Status"
  class="h-9 min-w-48 cursor-pointer rounded-lg border border-input bg-background px-3 text-sm [appearance:base-select] focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-ring [&::picker(select)]:[appearance:base-select] [&::picker(select)]:mt-1 [&::picker(select)]:min-w-48 [&::picker(select)]:scale-95 [&::picker(select)]:rounded-lg [&::picker(select)]:border [&::picker(select)]:border-border [&::picker(select)]:bg-popover [&::picker(select)]:p-1 [&::picker(select)]:text-popover-foreground [&::picker(select)]:opacity-0 [&::picker(select)]:shadow-lg [&::picker(select)]:transition-[opacity,scale,overlay,display] [&::picker(select)]:transition-discrete [&::picker(select)]:duration-150 [&:open::picker(select)]:scale-100 [&:open::picker(select)]:opacity-100 starting:[&:open::picker(select)]:scale-95 starting:[&:open::picker(select)]:opacity-0 [&::picker-icon]:text-muted-foreground [&::picker-icon]:transition-transform [&::picker-icon]:content-[var(--chevron)] [&::picker-icon]:relative [&::picker-icon]:top-0.5 [&::picker-icon]:duration-200 [&::picker-icon]:flex [&::picker-icon]:items-center"
>
  <button>
    <selectedcontent class="flex items-center gap-2"></selectedcontent>
  </button>
  <option
    value="active"
    class="flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-md px-2.5 py-1.5 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:order-1 [&::checkmark]:ml-auto [&::checkmark]:text-primary"
  >
    <span class="size-2 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-emerald-500" aria-hidden="true"></span> Active
  </option>
  <option
    value="paused"
    class="flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-md mt-0.5 px-2.5 py-1.5 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:order-1 [&::checkmark]:ml-auto [&::checkmark]:text-primary"
  >
    <span class="size-2 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-amber-500" aria-hidden="true"></span> Paused
  </option>
  <option
    value="archived"
    class="flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-md mt-0.5 px-2.5 py-1.5 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:order-1 [&::checkmark]:ml-auto [&::checkmark]:text-primary"
  >
    <span class="size-2 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-zinc-400" aria-hidden="true"></span> Archived
  </option>
</select>
```

## How it works

- `[appearance:base-select]` on **both** the `<select>` and its `::picker(select)` pseudo-element
  opts into the new customizable select rendering.
- Options can contain real HTML (the colored status dots). `<selectedcontent>` mirrors the
  chosen option's content into the closed button.
- `::picker(select)` is the dropdown panel: styled, animated with `transition-discrete`
  (`transition-behavior: allow-discrete`) + `starting:`, rendered in the top layer.
- `::picker-icon` is the chevron (rotates when open); `::checkmark` marks the selected option.

## Align the selection with the trigger

```html
<select
  aria-label="Fruit"
  class="h-9 min-w-48 cursor-pointer rounded-lg border border-input bg-background px-3 text-sm [--option-height:2.25rem] [appearance:base-select] focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-1 focus-visible:outline-ring [&::picker(select)]:[appearance:base-select] [&::picker(select)]:[position-area:auto] [&::picker(select)]:[align-self:auto] [&::picker(select)]:[position-try-fallbacks:none] [&::picker(select)]:[max-block-size:100vh] [&::picker(select)]:min-w-48 [&::picker(select)]:rounded-lg [&::picker(select)]:border [&::picker(select)]:border-border [&::picker(select)]:bg-popover [&::picker(select)]:p-0 [&::picker(select)]:text-popover-foreground [&::picker(select)]:opacity-0 [&::picker(select)]:shadow-lg [&::picker(select)]:transition-opacity [&::picker(select)]:duration-150 [&:open::picker(select)]:opacity-100 starting:[&:open::picker(select)]:opacity-0 [&:has(option:nth-of-type(1):checked)]:[--checked-index:1] [&:has(option:nth-of-type(2):checked)]:[--checked-index:2] [&:has(option:nth-of-type(3):checked)]:[--checked-index:3] [&:has(option:nth-of-type(4):checked)]:[--checked-index:4] [&:has(option:nth-of-type(5):checked)]:[--checked-index:5] [&:has(option:nth-of-type(6):checked)]:[--checked-index:6] [&::picker(select)]:[top:calc(var(--option-height)*-1*var(--checked-index,0))] [&::picker-icon]:relative [&::picker-icon]:top-0.5 [&::picker-icon]:flex [&::picker-icon]:items-center [&::picker-icon]:text-muted-foreground [&::picker-icon]:content-[var(--chevron)]"
>
  <button>
    <selectedcontent class="flex items-center gap-2"></selectedcontent>
  </button>
  <option
    value=""
    class="flex h-[var(--option-height)] cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 text-sm text-muted-foreground focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:hidden"
  >
    Select a fruit
  </option>
  <option
    value="apple"
    class="flex h-[var(--option-height)] cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:hidden"
  >
    Apple
  </option>
  <option
    value="banana"
    class="flex h-[var(--option-height)] cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:hidden"
  >
    Banana
  </option>
  <option
    value="blueberry"
    class="flex h-[var(--option-height)] cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:hidden"
  >
    Blueberry
  </option>
  <option
    value="grapes"
    class="flex h-[var(--option-height)] cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:hidden"
  >
    Grapes
  </option>
  <option
    value="pineapple"
    class="flex h-[var(--option-height)] cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 text-sm focus:bg-accent focus:outline-none [&::checkmark]:hidden"
  >
    Pineapple
  </option>
</select>
```

The classic OS popup menu move: instead of dropping below, the picker opens *over* the trigger with
your current choice already sitting under the cursor. It's a tiny bit of Fitts's-law generosity.
The option you're most likely to want is exactly where your pointer already is.

- Undo the UA placement: `position-area: auto`, `align-self: auto`, `position-try-fallbacks: none`,
  so the picker is a plain top-layer box we position by hand instead of an anchored dropdown.
- Give every `<option>` a fixed `--option-height` equal to the trigger's own height. The panel's
  default position sits just under the trigger, so nudging it up by `--checked-index × --option-height`
  pulls the selected row right onto the trigger.
- The catch: the picker can't read *which* option is checked (`sibling-index()` won't help: it only
  reports an element's own slot, never a descendant's). So it stays a `:has()` chain, one rule per
  option, on `nth-of-type` to skip the `<button>`. Keep it DRY by having each rule set a bare
  `--checked-index` and doing the math once: `top: calc(var(--option-height) * -1 * var(--checked-index, 0))`.
- Drop the `transition-discrete` (`allow-discrete`) that the basic select uses, and transition only
  `opacity`. Because the panel's `top` is recalculated from whichever option is checked, keeping the
  picker in the top layer through a discrete `display` exit lets it jump to the *new* selection's
  position mid-fade. Letting it close immediately gives a cleaner animate-out here.

## The fallback is the feature

In browsers without `base-select`, this renders as the **classic native select**. Ugly options,
maybe, but fully functional, accessible, and familiar. That's a much better floor than a
JS-powered listbox that breaks without hydration.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

- **Customizable select (appearance: base-select)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn_web_development/Extensions/Forms/Customizable_select))
  Without it: Falls back to the classic native select — fully functional and accessible, just not custom-styled.
- **Entry/exit animations (allow-discrete + @starting-style)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/transition-behavior))
  Without it: Elements appear and disappear instantly. Nothing breaks — you just lose the animation.
